description: Test corpus for the mobile-security skill. Fixtures are schema-driven Android/iOS snippets; 'detect' marks code that hardcodes secrets, stores sensitive data in plaintext, or disables TLS validation (violates a NEVER), and 'ignore' marks code using Keystore/Keychain and certificate pinning (follows ALWAYS).

fixtures.reason: An empty checkServerTrusted disables all TLS certificate validation, enabling MITM. CWE-295 Improper Certificate Validation; violates NEVER (custom TLS handling that returns 'trust all').

fixtures.text:
val prefs = getSharedPreferences("auth", Context.MODE_PRIVATE)
prefs.edit()
    .putString("api_key", "AIzaSyD-9fA2bQ7xR3kL8mN0pZ4tWvYuC1eHjGd")
    .putString("refresh_token", token)
    .apply()
fixtures.reason: Hard-codes an API key and stores a refresh token in plaintext SharedPreferences (readable on rooted/backed-up devices). CWE-312 Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information / CWE-798; violates NEVER (ship API keys in source; store secrets in plain SharedPreferences).

Stores an auth token and a hardcoded secret key in UserDefaults, which is an unencrypted plist readable from backups. CWE-312 Cleartext Storage; violates NEVER/ALWAYS (don't store secrets in UserDefaults; use the Keychain).

fixtures.text:
val pinner = CertificatePinner.Builder()
    .add("api.example.com", "sha256/AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA=")
    .build()
val client = OkHttpClient.Builder()
    .certificatePinner(pinner)
    .build()
fixtures.reason: Uses OkHttp CertificatePinner to pin the backend's public key, validating the server cert. Follows ALWAYS (certificate pinning via OkHttp.CertificatePinner); safe per CWE-295.

fixtures.text:
val masterKey = MasterKey.Builder(context)
    .setKeyScheme(MasterKey.KeyScheme.AES256_GCM)
    .build()
val prefs = EncryptedSharedPreferences.create(
    context, "secure_auth", masterKey,
    EncryptedSharedPreferences.PrefKeyEncryptionScheme.AES256_SIV,
    EncryptedSharedPreferences.PrefValueEncryptionScheme.AES256_GCM
)
prefs.edit().putString("refresh_token", token).apply()
fixtures.reason: Stores the refresh token in EncryptedSharedPreferences backed by an Android Keystore MasterKey (AES-256-GCM), not plaintext. Follows ALWAYS (store secrets in Android Keystore); safe per CWE-312.
Stores the auth token in the iOS Keychain with kSecAttrAccessibleAfterFirstUnlockThisDeviceOnly, the recommended secure store. Follows ALWAYS (store secrets in Keychain); safe per CWE-312.
